title: Weekly Surge In Google Trends Searches For Warehouse Worker Shortage Signals Labor Tightness
thesis: An increase in searches for warehouse worker shortage suggests rising labor scarcity in logistics-heavy sectors. Labor shortages in industrial sectors likely increase costs and disrupt supply chains.
signal_logic: If weekly searches for 'warehouse worker shortage' rise more than 15% over prior 4 weeks
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly searches for 'warehouse worker shortage' rise more than 15% over prior 4 weeks Exit: When searches decline below 10% above 4-week prior average
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search interest via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Labor tightness topics often fluctuate weekly and can spike due to seasonal demand or economic shifts.

schema.idea: Weekly surge in Google Trends searches for 'warehouse worker shortage' signals labor tightness
schema.behavior: An increase in searches for warehouse worker shortage suggests rising labor scarcity in logistics-heavy sectors.

market_impact.rationale: Labor shortages in industrial sectors likely increase costs and disrupt supply chains.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly searches for 'warehouse worker shortage' rise more than 15% over prior 4 weeks
trade_logic.exit: When searches decline below 10% above 4-week prior average

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Labor tightness topics often fluctuate weekly and can spike due to seasonal demand or economic shifts.
